title: DMK MPs urges Centre to constitute tribunal for Mekedatu
content: “The Mettur Dam could not be opened because of Karnataka’s recalcitrant stand. This has caused enormous hardship to farmers,” a resolution adopted at the meeting said

title: Heatwave-like conditions persist in Central A.P., a few areas receive light to moderate showers
content: Cloudy sky and light to moderate rain predicted for some parts of the State on Friday under the influence of a well-marked low pressure over Northwest Bay of Bengal

title: Tribal farmer producer company from Idukki launches drinking water brand ‘Anjunad'
content: Brand launched by Girijyothi Farmer Producer Company, which is an initiative of the High Range Development Society and comprises over 700 tribal farmers from the Anchunad hills

title: Govt. keen on strengthening handloom and handicraft sectors, says Minister Savitha
content: The government will continue to support artisans by expanding market access, encouraging innovation, while preserving traditional skills and facilitating participation in national and international exhibitions, she says

title: RJD spokesperson Mrityunjay Tiwari resigns from his post
content: “There is no point to remain in the party after being insulted repeatedly… I‘ve resigned from all posts of the party and the party itself”

title: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu flags off Jagannath Rath Yatra in Amaravati, says it will be an annual affair
content: Human relationships are weakening and negative influence of social media increasing, he observes, saying that spirituality offers the path to mental peace and emotional well-being

title: Pothundy double murder: Chenthamara’s sentencing deferred to Monday
content: The prosecution has sought the death penalty for Chenthamara, while the defence has requested consideration of life imprisonment and other lesser punishments

title: Police arrest, in all, 12 in several cases in Kalaburagi
content: They have recovered stolen property, including gold ornaments, cash, cattle and a vehicle, in all valued at over ₹15 lakh

title: Kerala Higher Education Minister urges MPs to oppose VBSA Bill
content: Roji M. John says proposed law undermines federalism, academic autonomy and State powers in higher education

title: Daily ridership of women passengers on KSRTC Ordinary buses increase from 6.48 lakh to 12.12 lakh, says Kerala Minister
content: Despite the roll-out of the scheme on Ordinary buses, number of male passengers using these services has remained almost constant. This indicates that increase in women passengers has not come at the expense of other passengers, says C.P. John

title: Two die as lakhs throng Jagannath Yatra in Puri
content: The stampede-like situation occurred when devotees were pushed aside to make way for an ambulance moving through the crowd; of around a hundred devotees admitted for treatment, the condition of two remains critical

title: ₹10,120 cr. cleared for Thalliki Vandanam, 67.5 lakh students to get financial aid in A.P.
content: The first phase of disbursement to verified beneficiaries is scheduled for July 22, 2026, along with the publication of eligible and ineligible beneficiary lists

title: CM Vijay warns T.N. Cabinet colleagues against corruption and irregularities in depts.
content: Sources said the Chief Minister, chairing a Cabinet meeting, made it clear that he would not hesitate to take action against anyone found guilty and that no one could later fault him for doing so

title: Food adulteration, cyber fraud and drugs under focus in Hyderabad: Sajjanar
content: As Hyderabad continues to expand beyond its old boundaries, policing the city increasingly involves coordinating with multiple government agencies, responding to changing crime patterns and adapting to new technologies. In an interview with The Hindu, Hyderabad City Police Commissioner V.C. Sajjanar discusses the challenges of managing a rapidly growing metropolis.

title: The Hindu Impact: NGT initiates suo motu proceedings in Kerala’s Chalakkudi tree-felling case
content: Trees felled along an 18-km stretch from Vellikulangara Junction to Vettila Pala bridge and sold for ₹8.52 lakh, against the assessed value of ₹41.55 lakh

title: Police arrest two, seize firearms in Bidar; search on for two more
content: Five country-made pistols, 26 live cartridges, 10 empty cartridge cases and five swords recovered from three bags being carried by the accused

title: Bengaluru’s tunnel road project subject to outcome of petitions, says Karnataka High Court
content: The High Court of Karnataka directed that any contract entered into by the State government or its agencies, with third parties for the proposed tunnel road project, will be subject to the outcome of the hearing on three PIL petitions challenging the legality of the approvals for the project

title: Right to privacy activists demand proposed unified card be kept in abeyance
content: Any move to create a unique identity number, collect data on every resident and link it to a single profile requires legislation, right to privacy activists argue

title: IUML faces Waqf dilemma as UDF govt. opts to follow amended law
content: The party opposes the amended Waqf Act that requires appointment of two non-Muslim members and one Shia member on State Waqf Boards. However, the UDF, of which the IUML is a major constituent, tells High Court that it will reconstitute the board in accordance with the Act

title: Police arrest Kolhapur man, solve woman’s murder in Belagavi
content: The accused has confessed to committing the crime as the woman was urging him to return the ₹1.5 lakh she had given as hand loan, say police

title: Flu, H1N1 rampant in Kerala; 4,405 cases of influenza reported
content: Surveillance of all influenza-like illnesses and severe acute respiratory infections has been intensified across districts and the treatment protocol and guidelines have been revisited

title: ITI bags 4G expansion order worth ₹856 cr. from BSNL
content: The city-based PSU has been executing successfully and has proved to be an able ally in the country’s Atmanirbhar telecom initiatives: Rajesh Rai, CMD, ITI Ltd.

title: 'Pahandi' procession, choked Grand Road : What triggered crowd scare at Puri Rath Yatra
content: During Puri's Rath Yatra, a sudden surge in the crowd resulted in a tragic death of one devotee. More than one hundred individuals were treated for suffocation, prompting emergency responders to evacuate some on stretchers near the Singhadwara. This event unfolded as thousands attended the Pahandi ritual and chariot procession, reigniting important discussions about crowd safety following last year's similar incident.

title: Channi vs Warring in Punjab: Why Congress may not give in to pressure politics
content: Congress leadership struggles to unite Punjab leaders amid ongoing internal disputes. New leadership structure has triggered open conflict and fresh rounds of infighting. Former Chief Minister Charanjit Singh Channi is now leading a revolt against the current party president. The party leadership appears firm this time, avoiding past mistakes of succumbing to pressure. Managing these divisions is critical for Congress to regain political ground in Punjab.

title: Sonakshi Sinha, Fatima Sana Shaikh and Imran Khan back Sonam Wangchuk’s hunger strike
content: As Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike reaches its nineteenth day, prominent film industry figures rally behind him. Sonakshi Sinha and Fatima Sana Shaikh are leading a call for authorities to engage in constructive dialogue. Imran Khan has also raised his voice, demanding accountability in light of the NEET-UG controversy. Many more voices from the film world have echoed similar sentiments, advocating for discussions.

title: Larry Ellison and David Ellison sued by Paramount investors for 'government connection'
content: A shareholder lawsuit accuses Larry and David Ellison of a corrupt deal. They allegedly promised private benefits to President Trump for regulatory approval. This deal included firing CNN anchors Trump disliked. Paramount denies these allegations, calling them recycled and baseless claims. The company asserts the merger's economic merits will benefit consumers.

title: 'Make Wangchuk education minister': Kejriwal joins protest, seeks Pradhan's ouster
content: Arvind Kejriwal joined Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike at Jantar Mantar. He demanded the removal of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Kejriwal also endorsed Wangchuk as a potential replacement for the minister. He highlighted recurring exam paper leaks and student suicides. The former Delhi CM urged the government to listen to youth concerns.

title: Rajasthan school teacher 'strip-searches' students over missing Rs 500, suspended
content: A teacher in Rajasthan was suspended following serious complaints from villagers. Locals alleged students were strip-searched after money went missing from a classroom. However, officials investigated and found these allegations to be entirely false. Only student bags were checked by other students, not the teacher herself. The department suspended the teacher to protect its image despite the false claims.

title: Jeff Bezos donates $10m to LA that city's mayor says is about investing in culture
content: Jeff Bezos' Earth Fund has granted $10.1 million for Watts Towers Arts Center Campus improvements. This investment will expand usable park space and introduce new cooling features. The project aims to strengthen the campus as a cultural hub before the 2028 Olympics. Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass welcomed the significant milestone for the community. This funding advances commitments to green space and climate resilience efforts.

title: Cyberattack hits Japan's ice cream giant Glico, disrupts sushi & supermarket deliveries
content: A cyberattack on Nichirei has disrupted ice cream and fast food supplies across Japan. Popular companies like Ezaki Glico and KFC Japan are experiencing significant product delays. Some restaurants may face temporary menu changes and potential store closures due to shortages. This incident follows other recent cyberattacks targeting major Japanese corporations. The government urges companies to update systems and strengthen cybersecurity measures.

title: Horses watched silent wolf videos, their hearts raced without visible fear
content: Horses exhibit an intriguing phenomenon when identifying predators on video screens—they experience heightened heart rates indicative of internal stress while showing no outward signs of fear. Notably, male horses and dominant herd members trigger stronger responses, challenging traditional views of prey animals' panic-driven behavior. Grasping this psychological disconnect is essential for improving horse welfare and ensuring rider safety.

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide: Expected specifications, price in India and launch date
content: Samsung's Galaxy Unpacked event on July 22 will likely unveil three new foldable phones. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide is expected to feature a large 7.6-inch inner display. This new model will offer a 5.5-inch outer cover screen and dual rear cameras. It is anticipated to launch with 12GB RAM and multiple storage options.

title: Indralok Aawasiy Yojana 2 BHK flats: Eligibility, price, registration fee and lottery rules
content: Uttarakhand residents can now own homes through the Indralok Aawasiy Yojana in Haridwar.  This scheme offers two categories of flats for different income groups.  Registration requires a ten percent deposit and a nominal processing fee.  Applications are verified before a transparent lottery selects beneficiaries for these homes.  Successful applicants can pay in full or use an instalment plan.

title: Virat Kohli breaks Ricky Ponting's record, enters all-time top-five elite list
content: Virat Kohli achieved a significant career milestone during the second ODI against England. He surpassed Ricky Ponting to become the fifth-most capped player in international cricket. Kohli scored 65 runs, contributing to India's strong position in the match. This half-century also drew him level with Viv Richards for fifty-plus scores against England. His remarkable longevity continues to be a defining aspect of his career.

title: 100 imports, $51bn bill: India steps up domestic manufacturing
content: The renewed focus on expanding domestic production comes as India faces heightened supply-chain risks arising from geopolitical tensions. At the same time, the government is seeking to lower its dependence on China while reducing the country's trade deficit.

title: Why the Maple Leafs may rethink Auston Matthews' $53M future
content: Toronto Maple Leafs captain Auston Matthews is once again in the spotlight as fresh NHL trade rumors have emerged following the team’s offseason moves. Matthews has not asked for a trade, so there is no indication he is itching to leave. But it has been speculated that the club’s performance during the 2026-27 NHL season could go a long way toward impacting talks about his long-term future ahead of a pivotal contract time frame.

title: 'Cabinet expansion very soon': Cong hints at Karnataka reshuffle after Rahul-DKS meeting
content: Karnataka cabinet expansion is expected to occur very soon. Senior Congress leaders met in Delhi to discuss the long-pending cabinet appointments. Consultations are underway to fill twenty vacant ministerial positions and the Speaker's post. The party aims to balance regional and caste equations for the selections. This expansion is anticipated before the upcoming assembly session begins in August.

title: 'No better person': Ashwin backs MS Dhoni to succeed Fleming as CSK coach
content: Ravichandran Ashwin discussed Chennai Super Kings' new chapter after Stephen Fleming's departure. He believes MS Dhoni would be the ideal choice to lead the team. Ashwin noted the exceptional Fleming-Dhoni partnership that brought significant success. He also highlighted the challenges for any new coach due to Dhoni's influence. The franchise faces a crucial decision shaping its future direction.

title: South Bengaluru horror: Man strangles pregnant wife with dog's leash, dies by suicide
content: A man allegedly strangled his pregnant wife to death with a dog's leash. He then died by suicide at their rented house in Banashankari Park. The house owner alerted police after hearing loud arguments and screams. Police discovered the wife's body in the bedroom and the husband hanging. Investigations are ongoing to determine the motive behind the tragic incident.

title: MIT built a 300-gram robot that flies through the sky, dives underwater and takes off again
content: The Flapping-wing Aerial-aquatic Vehicle (FAAV), developed by engineers at MIT and EPFL, is a 300-gram robot designed to mimic the diving birds like the Atlantic puffin. It is the first machine capable of completing the full cycle of swimming, diving, and launching back into the sky using  its wings rather than separate propellers or legs.

title: Watch: Argentina fans burn England flags after FIFA WC semi-final win
content: Argentina's World Cup semi-final win over England was overshadowed by controversial celebrations, with some fans burning England flags, displaying St George's Cross coffins and chanting Falklands War songs. Players also posed with a “Las Malvinas son Argentinas” banner after the match. President Javier Milei reiterated Argentina's claim, saying, “Sovereignty is not negotiable”.

title: H-1B returnees face tough Indian IT market with lower pay, fewer jobs
content: Recruitment trends indicate that companies are hiring cautiously, and many returnees are getting jobs at less than their salary expectations. Artificial intelligence has compounded the challenge since many roles have become redundant.

title: Capital of contrasts: While Delhi moves on, Kirari remains under water
content: Kirari residents endure sewage-laden water and illness after recent heavy monsoon rains. Children cannot attend flooded schools which remain submerged for days. Public parks have become filthy lakes, and homes are inundated with water. Locals navigate streets using makeshift pathways, facing chronic civic neglect. Authorities are constructing drainage systems to resolve the recurring waterlogging issue.

title: India's real estate equity inflows hit record $8.5 billion in H1 2026: CBRE
content: India's real estate sector achieved record equity capital inflows in early 2025. Land and office asset acquisitions fueled this significant growth in capital. Domestic investors showed strong conviction, contributing most of the investment inflows. The momentum is expected to continue through the year. Global capital is anticipated to return as conditions stabilize.

title: 'Those under pressure, go wherever you want': Mamata as TMC exodus continues
content: Mamata Banerjee addressed the Trinamool Congress exodus after the recent assembly poll defeat. She suggested leaders facing pressure are free to leave the party. Rajya Sabha MP Koel Mallick resigned, adding to the growing list of exits. This resignation followed Madan Mitra quitting to join a rebel TMC faction. Banerjee also defended her nephew Abhishek Banerjee amid internal party issues.

title: ICC reprimands Indian cricketer for throwing ball in 'dangerous manner' ahead of 2nd ODI
content: India fast bowler Gurnoor Brar was warned by the ICC and given one demerit point for breaching the Code of Conduct during the first ODI against England after throwing the ball towards Ben Duckett in a manner deemed "inappropriate and dangerous." Despite the incident, Brar took two wickets as India won by six wickets to lead the three-match series 1-0.

title: Low-cost smart munitions and cruise missiles, is this the future of combat?
content: Modern conflicts highlight the need for precision weapons and their scalable deployment. High-end precision munitions prove prohibitively expensive in prolonged engagements. Nations are developing cheaper alternatives to sustain extended combat operations. The US Air Force plans thousands of affordable cruise missiles for future conflicts. This shift prioritizes affordability and rapid production for modern warfare demands.

title: 'Manmohan govt engaged with Anna Hazare ... ': Omar says Centre unmoved by Wangchuk
content: Jammu and Kashmir chief minister Omar Abdullah supports Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike. Wangchuk is protesting alleged irregularities in the NEET examination, demanding accountability. His health has significantly deteriorated after nineteen days of fasting. Doctors warn of serious health complications if the hunger strike continues. The Delhi High Court has ordered daily medical examinations for Wangchuk.

title: Renting a home without a written agreement? Here's why it may be risky
content: In India, verbal rental agreements often lead to misunderstandings, as they lack essential written terms and legal safeguards. This scenario exposes tenants to unpredictable rent increases and abrupt evictions. The absence of documentation complicates security deposits, and property repairs frequently result in disputes. To protect both parties, written contracts are now a requirement under the Model Tenancy Act in states that have adopted it.

title: 'Mirzapur: The Movie' director Gurmmeet Singh says film is aiming for an 'A' certificate
content: Mirzapur The Movie targets an Adults Only certification, aiming to retain its intense identity. Director Gurmmeet Singh believes audiences are receptive to mature theatrical content now. The film condenses the sprawling series while maintaining its core essence and narrative focus. Familiar faces return, but audiences can expect unexpected twists and evolving character dynamics. The highly anticipated Mirzapur The Movie is scheduled for release on September 4, 2026.

title: China charms world, now more popular than US; Xi tops Trump on trust: Report
content: Recent global surveys reveal that China's image is increasingly favorable compared to that of the United States. Under Xi Jinping's leadership, he commands greater trust internationally than Donald Trump does. While personal freedoms remain a strength for the US, this advantage is slowly diminishing as China is perceived to engage less in interventionist policies worldwide. These trends underscore a noteworthy shift in international perceptions.

title: Simone Biles shares ESPYS 2026 BTS with Suni Lee after comeback announcement
content: Simone Biles and Suni Lee grabbed attention both on and off the red carpet at the 2026 ESPYS in New York City. The two Olympic gymnastics stars impressed fans with their stylish outfits, while Biles also shared behind-the-scenes moments on her Instagram Story. Their appearance quickly became one of the biggest talking points from the annual sports awards ceremony.

title: 'Lesson for playing with his life': How secret marriage led to Bengaluru student's murder
content: Two brothers allegedly threatened a woman before she was fatally stabbed at her Bengaluru home.  The victim, Amrutha, died from her injuries two days after the brutal attack occurred.  Police arrested brothers Dhanush and Surya in connection with the violent murder case.  Amrutha ended her relationship with Dhanush after discovering his prior marriage and child.  The dispute between the families reportedly escalated after the breakup, leading to this tragedy.

title: IND vs ENG: Why is KL Rahul not playing in India vs England 2nd ODI at Cardiff?
content: KL Rahul missed India's second ODI against England in Cardiff due to illness, with Ishan Kishan replacing him in the playing XI. Confirming the change at the toss, captain Shubman Gill said, "KL is sick and Ishan comes in for him." England won the toss and chose to bowl, while India lead the three-match series 1-0.

title: Falkland poster row spills over: British govt seeks FIFA action against Argentina
content: Argentina defeated England 2-1 in the FIFA World Cup semifinal on Wednesday in Atlanta. During the post-match celebrations, Argentine players posed with a banner handed to them by supporters in the stands that read, "Las Malvinas son Argentinas" ("The Malvinas are Argentine"). Argentina refers to the Falkland Islands as Islas Malvinas.

title: Marriage certified by a notary? MP HC says they have no such power
content: The Madhya Pradesh high court suspended a notary's license for preparing a fake marriage document. The court ruled that notaries lack authority to function as marriage officers. A young woman was freed after alleging coercion into a sham court marriage. She expressed her desire to return to her parents and continue studies. The court ordered further investigation into the notary's actions.

title: 'Convene an all-party meeting': Kharge writes to PM Modi on revised Delimitation Bill
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to call an all-party meeting. He requested adequate time for opposition parties to study the revised delimitation proposals. The Congress party plans to strongly oppose the proposed delimitation bill if reintroduced. They will also work to maintain unity among all opposition parties. The party supports women's reservation but opposes linking it to delimitation.

title: 'Wanted papers': Drunk son kills father over Rs 150cr property dispute in Ghaziabad
content: A 32-year-old man allegedly shot his father dead after a heated argument. The dispute began when the father confronted his son for returning home drunk. A land dispute was also ongoing between the father and his elder son. The son then fired multiple bullets at his father, killing him instantly. Police have registered a murder case and are searching for the accused.

title: Kannada social media influencer booked for sexual harassment in Bengaluru
content: According to the complaint, the alleged incident took place during the promotion of a pub, where Manju had reportedly consumed alcohol and behaved inappropriately with a female server

title: Bombay HC asks Centre to file reply on Kunal Kamra’s plea
content: The stand-up comedian has challenged the constitutional validity of Sahyog Portal and the 2025 amendment to Rule 3(1)(d) of the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021

title: Thousands participate in Jagannath Rath Yatra across Hyderabad
content: The annual Rath Yatra, drawing inspiration from Puri Rath Yatra, commemorates the deities’ annual sojourn out of the temple to bless devotees

title: Naidu stresses need for PPP–Viability Gap Funding Policy for infra development in A.P.
content: Chief Minister directs officials to place the AP Public-Private Partnership–VGF Policy 1.0 before the next Cabinet meeting and to issue detailed implementation guidelines at the earliest

title: Ammonia safety drive prompts major overhaul in Guntur cold storages
content: Nearly 70 out of 100 facilities have relocated ammonia storage tanks from compressor rooms to safer outdoor locations, significantly reducing the risk of industrial disasters

title: Two arrested with 237 kg marijuana in Hyderabad
content: EAGLE has seized around 1,500 kg of marijuana being transported to different parts of the country in the last two months as part of its intensified surveillance on Odisha-based supply routes

title: M.M. Mani criticises forest, police depts. for ‘not abiding by law’
content: Former Minister alleges that Forest dept. is encroaching on revenue land at Kanjikuzhi. He says if officials evict people, party workers will take the law into their own hands

title: Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’ release forces Tamil films to reconsider release plans
content: The announcement of the release of Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’ has forced a slew of smaller Tamil films to reconsider or reschedule their releases

title: On Camera, Man Attacks Woman With Machete At Karnataka Bus Stand. She Dies
content: Preliminary information suggests the accused pulled out a machete from his bag, following which the woman tried to flee. The accused chased her, attacked her with the weapon, and fled the scene.

title: Court Orders Framing Charges In Cross-Border Narco-Terror Funding Case
content: A Special NIA Court has ordered the framing of charges against 10 accused in a cross-border narco-terror case, paving the way for trial of an alleged syndicate that smuggled drugs into Jammu and Kashmir to fund terrorist activities.

title: 30 Lakh Investors Cheated Of Rs 10,000 Crore. Then A Key Arrest
content: The case pertains to alleged cheating, criminal breach of trust and misappropriation of investors' money amounting to approximately Rs 10,314 crore.

title: "Trying To Change Public Opinion": JD Vance On Israel's Influence In Iran Deal
content: JD Vance said that there are some people within their system that are manipulating the American public opinion to keep the war going on indefinitely.

title: Monsoon Session Likely To Bring 5 New Bills And 2 Pending Ones
content: There has been speculation the government will proceed with Constitution Amendment Bills on delimitation and women's reservation only after it is confident of securing the requisite two-thirds majority

title: Assam's Monkey Menace, Human-Elephant Conflict Trigger SOP To Handle Them
content: 2025 recorded the highest annual human death count, with 138 lives lost, while electrocution and train accidents remained among the leading causes of elephant deaths.

title: "Sonam Wangchuk Should Be Education Minister": Arvind Kejriwal
content: Arvind Kejriwal made the remarks on Thursday while visiting Sonam Wangchuk at Jantar Mantar, where the latter is leading a protest over alleged irregularities in national examinations, particularly the NEET medical entrance test.

title: 'Take Off Your Uniform And Fight Elections': Pakistan MP Dares Asim Munir
content: Addressing a public gathering in Pakistan on Sunday, Jamiat Ulema-e-Islam-F (JUI-F) chief Maulana Fazlur Rehman said, "If you want to do politics, then take off your uniform and come. Take part in the election. You will get to know how many votes people in uniform receive."

title: Fresh Floods, Landslides Hit 5 Districts In Arunachal Pradesh
content: Fresh floods and landslides were reported in Arunachal Pradesh, damaging houses, roads and crops, officials said on Thursday.

title: 1 Dead, Several Taken To Hospital After Crowd Surge At Puri Rath Yatra
content: One person died and several others were injured after a crowd surge at the iconic Rath Yatra celebrations in Odisha's Puri on Thursday.

title: Posed As IPS Officer, Spy And Soldier: Con Of Top Bureaucrat's Son Ends
content: Yashvardhan, the son of former Uttarakhand Chief Secretary S Ramaswami, was arrested on Thursday during a routine check on the Mussoorie Road.

title: "Everyone Does Naarebaazi In Democracy": Top Court Relief For Bhagwant Man
content: A bench of Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, Justice Joymalya Bagchi and Justice V Mohanawas was hearing the Chandigarh administration's challenge to the Punjab and Haryana High Court order

title: Man Beats Mother, 65, To Death After Argument In Indore, Arrested
content: The incident took place at the family's residence in Niranjanpur Basti under the jurisdiction of Lasudia police station in the district on Wednesday.

title: 5 Of Family Killed After Scooter Crashes Into Parked Tractor In Chhattisgarh
content: The accident occurred under Kumhari police station limits adjoining Raipur when the five family members were travelling on the scooter.

title: 'Operations On Track': OnePlus Junks Report Of Quitting India
content: Junking a report on Thursday, the company told NDTV Profit that OnePlus India continues to operate its business as usual.

title: Mumbai's Taj Hotel Issued Rs 22 Crore Notice Over 26/11 Security Barricades
content: The civic body's action targets the heavy security barricades, bollards, and planters erected around the hotel's perimeter in June 2009 following the 26/11 terror attacks.

title: Karnataka Cabinet Reshuffle Delayed, High-Level Meetings Continue In Delhi
content: Amid the delay, a series of back to back meetings took place in New Delhi on Thursday, involving top Congress leaders from Karnataka and the party high command.

title: 2 Brothers Arrested For Beheading Thane Man, Filming Murder On Phone
content: A probe began after a case was registered at Mumbra police station under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita sections pertaining to murder, destruction of evidence and other offences, police said.

title: Delhi May Lease 'Sheesh Mahal' To Luxury Hotel, Keep It Open For State Events
content: The residence will remain available whenever it is needed for official conferences, functions or meetings. During vacant periods, it could be rented out for private programmes, allowing the government to earn revenue while reducing its maintenance expenditure.

title: Man Shoots Woman Dead, Then Dies By Suicide In Delhi Flat
content: Police said they received a PCR call about two people lying dead in a flat, rushed to the spot, and launched an investigation.

title: Brains without borders: US central bank picks merit over MAGA hysteria
content: five task forces are charged with rethinking the central bank's approach to monetary policy . they propose "transformational changes" to prepare the institution for a rapidly changing economy . former central bankers, academics and corporate executives tapped to lead the effort .

title: Inside David Beckham's empire: From 'Beckingham Palace' to $72 million Miami mansion
content: The TOI Real Estate Desk is a focused team of seasoned journalists and market watchers dedicated to decoding the ever-evolving property landscape for The Times of India readers. With a sharp eye on trends, policy shifts, and market movements, the team brings clarity to one of the most significant investment decisions in people’s lives.
